sections.paragraph:
m. with suff. סִפְרִי, pl. סְפָרִים, constr. סִפְרֵי.-

(1) writing, Arab. سفْرُ, Syr. ܣܶܦܪܐܳ-(a) the art of writing and reading, Isa 29:11, 12 Isa 29:12, יֹודֵעַ הַסֵּפֶר “one acquainted with writing.”-(b) a kind of writing, Dan 1:4, סֵפֶר וּלְשֹׁון בַּשְׂדִּים “the writing (letters) and the tongue of the Chaldeans;” verse 17 .

(2) a writing, whatever is written, used of a bill of sale, Jer 32:12, seq.; of a charge or accusation, Job 31:35 of a bill of divorce, Deu 24:1, Deu 24:3of a letter, 2Sa 11:14 2Ki 10:1.

(3) a book, Exo 17:14. סֵפֶר הַתֹּורָה the book of the law, Jos 1:8 8:34 סֵפֶר הַבְּרִית the book of the covenant, Exo 24:7 2Ki 23:2 which is also called poetically מְגִלַּת סֵפֶר the volume of the book, Psal. 40:8 and κατʼ ἐξοχὴν סֵפֶר Isa 29:18, as in Arab. اكِتَابُ a book, specially the Koran. סֵפֶר חַיִּים the book of life, God’s index of the living, Psa 69:29 compare Dan 12:1 Rev 20:12, 15 . הַסְּפָרִים Dan 9:2 (holy) books, τὰ γράμματα. עֲשֹׂות סְפָרִים to make books, used in contempt of bookwrights, Ecc 12:12.
